model_context.series_context: Western Conference Semifinals, Spurs even series 1-1 after Game 2

game.venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

source_notes.official_book: NBA official scorer report uploaded by user: 20260506_MINSAS_book (1).pdf
source_notes.recap: AP recap supplied by user: Wembanyama and Spurs rebound to hand Timberwolves largest postseason defeat, 133-95, to even series.
source_notes.injury_context: Donte DiVincenzo inactive for Minnesota. Anthony Edwards played limited minutes off bench while returning from left knee issue.

market_positions.selection: No - San Antonio wins by over 10.5 points

turning_points: Spurs closed the 1st quarter up 24-17 after Minnesota shot 29.2% and committed 6 turnovers.
San Antonio won the 2nd quarter 35-18, creating a 59-35 halftime lead and effectively flipping the game into blowout regime.
Minnesota was held to 35 first-half points on 14-of-47 FG and 2-of-15 from three.
Spurs expanded the lead to 98-63 by the end of the 3rd quarter, triggering bench/game-state distortion for player props and totals.
Both teams combined for 67 points in the 4th quarter despite the game being noncompetitive, causing the live Under 206.5 equivalent to fail.
San Antonio's biggest lead reached 47; final margin settled at 38.

Victor Wembanyama.model_relevance: Aggressive offensive tone-setter; 14 points and 9 rebounds by halftime. Sat most of 4Q due to blowout.

De'Aaron Fox.model_relevance: Attack-mode trigger; hit both 3PT attempts after missing all 4 in Game 1. Pace pressure was a major Spurs multiplier.

Stephon Castle.model_relevance: Guard downhill pressure and foul generation created early separation.

Dylan Harper.model_relevance: Bench guard creation sustained Spurs run and prevented Minnesota stabilization.

Julian Champagnie.model_relevance: Third-quarter spacing spike; hit four threes in the decisive expansion segment.

Julius Randle.model_relevance: Rebound over failed because blowout cut minutes and turnover/foul pressure reduced rebounding posture.

Anthony Edwards.model_relevance: Bench/minutes restriction remained active; limited ability to stabilize Minnesota offense.

Rudy Gobert.model_relevance: Rebounding production existed, but offensive limitations and free throw misses fed Spurs runouts.

min_plus_10_5_equivalent.edge_quality: Failed due to complete blowout regime and Minnesota offensive collapse.

randle_7_plus_rebounds.edge_quality: Miss; Game 1 rebound-chance logic was overridden by reduced minutes and blowout bench pull.

live_total_read.edge_quality: Miss; garbage-time scoring spike plus Spurs bench efficiency overrode blowout under logic.

failure_modes: Minnesota entered offensive collapse by halftime: 35 points, 29.8% FG, 13.3% 3PT, 11 turnovers.
Spurs pace/transition pressure stayed live despite blowout, producing 29 fast-break points.
San Antonio shot 41.0% from three and 50.0% FG overall, including bench-driven 4Q scoring.
Minnesota committed 22 turnovers and shot only 51.6% from the free throw line, eliminating comeback or spread-cover paths.
Randle's rebound prop was vulnerable to blowout minutes compression; he played only 27:24 and finished with 5 rebounds.
The live under failed because 4Q bench units produced 67 combined points after the competitive phase was already over.
what_model_got_right: Correctly identified Randle as a rebound-channel candidate from Game 1 tracking, but failed to adequately price blowout minutes risk.
Correctly recognized first-half Minnesota offensive weakness at halftime for the live under setup.
Correctly tracked Edwards minutes restriction as a destabilizer for Minnesota.
patch_recommendations: Add BLOWOUT_PROP_MINUTES_KILL_SWITCH for rebound overs when spread expands beyond 20 before halftime or 28 before end of 3Q.
Add GARBAGE_TIME_TOTAL_OVER_SPIKE gate when bench units combine high 3PA rate with low defensive resistance in blowouts.
Increase penalty on under positions when pre-halftime total is low but favorite pace remains attack-oriented and bench shooters are efficient.
Add GAME2_HOME_FAVORITE_REBOUND_RESPONSE overlay when elite favorite loses Game 1 at home and early stars show attack-mode intent.
Downgrade underdog spread positions when opponent's first-half fast-break points plus turnover edge signal transition avalanche.

betting_result_summary.notes: Pregame MIN +10.5 equivalent, Randle 7+ rebounds, and live Under 206.5 equivalent all lost. Core failure was blowout-regime misclassification: San Antonio's Game 2 response created early separation, compressed Minnesota starter minutes, and still generated high 4Q scoring through bench units.
